description: An 82-year-old woman was allegedly mauled to death by her pet dog pitbull in Uttar Pradesh's Lucknow. The incident took place while the woman, a retired teacher, was alone in the house in Kaiserbagh locality. Her body was later found in a poll of blood by her son.The woman was rushed to the hospital where she succumbed to injuries due to excessive loss of blood, according to a report by news agency IANS.Police said the deceased woman with her 25-year-old son, a gym trainer. They own two pet dogs -- a pit bull and a Labrador. The neighbours told the police that when the son was away on work, they heard the dogs barking and the cries of Savitri."When we heard the old lady crying for help, we rushed out to their door but it was locked from inside and aunty was lying in a pool of blood. We tried to open the door but it was locked. We immediately informed her son," they said.The son rushed back home and with the help of neighbours, took his mother on a two-wheeler to Balrampur hospital from where she was referred to the KGMU trauma centre. Senior doctors at the trauma centre said that the woman had multiple deep wounds from neck to abdomen and her legs.The dog had sunk its teeth in the body of the deceased and the abdomen flesh was torn apart. Senior doctors said they tried their best but could not save the woman who succumbed due to excessive blood loss. The post-mortem examination was conducted later in the evening. The incident spread panic in the area. Locals said that the two dogs have been with the family for the past three years but never saw them turning hostile. It is not yet known what made the dog so hostile.

description: ರೈತರಿಗೆ ಬರ ಪರಿಹಾರ ನೀಡಲು ಹಣವಿಲ್ಲ ಎಂದು ಕುಂಟು ನೆಪ ಹೇಳುವ ಕಾಂಗ್ರೆಸ್, ಕನ್ನಡಿಗರ ತೆರಿಗೆ ಹಣದಲ್ಲಿ ಪುಟಗಟ್ಟಲೆ ರಾಜಕೀಯ ಜಾಹೀರಾತು ನೀಡಿ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ ಬೊಕ್ಕಸವನ್ನ ಲೂಟಿ ಮಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದೆ.2010-11 ರಿಂದ 2014-15 ರ ಅವಧಿಯಲ್ಲಿ 13 ನೇ ಹಣಕಾಸು ಆಯೋಗದಿಂದ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕವು *61,691 ಕೋಟಿ* ರೂ ಪಡೆದಿದೆ.14ನೇ ಹಣಕಾಸು ಆಯೋಗದಲ್ಲಿ 2015-16 ರಿಂದ 2019-20ರ ಅವಧಿಯಲ್ಲಿ*ಕರ್ನಾಟಕವು 1,51,309 ಕೋಟಿ ರೂ ಪಡೆದಿದೆ.*ಪ್ರಸ್ತುತ 15 ನೇ ಹಣಕಾಸು ಆಯೋಗ 2020-21 ರಿಂದ 31 ಮಾರ್ಚ್ 2026 ರವರೆಗೆ, ನಾಲ್ಕು ವರ್ಷಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕವು ಈಗಾಗಲೇ *1,29,854* ಕೋಟಿ ರೂ ಪಡೆದಿದೆ.ಐದನೇ ವರ್ಷದ ಅಂತ್ಯಕ್ಕೆ*ಕರ್ನಾಟಕಕ್ಕೆ 1,74,339 ಕೋಟಿ* ರೂಪಾಯಿ ಸಿಗಲಿದೆ ಎಂದು ಅಂದಾಜಿಸಲಾಗಿದೆ, ಇದು ತೆರಿಗೆ ಹಂಚಿಕೆ.ಅನುದಾನದಲ್ಲಿ 10 ವರ್ಷಗಳ ಯುಪಿಎಯಲ್ಲಿ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕಕ್ಕೆ *60,779.84 ಕೋಟಿ ರೂ.* ಮೋದಿ ಸರ್ಕಾರದ 9 ವರ್ಷಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕಕ್ಕೆ*2,08,832.02* ಕೋಟಿ ರೂ ಕ್ಕಿಂತ ಹೆಚ್ಚಾಗಿ, ಕರ್ನಾಟಕಕ್ಕೆ ಬಿಡುಗಡೆ ಮಾಡಲಾಗಿದೆ.*18,005 ಕೋಟಿ* ರೂಪಾಯಿಗಳ ಹೆಚ್ಚುವರಿ ಅನುದಾನವನ್ನು ಈ ವರ್ಷ ಬಜೆಟ್ ನಲ್ಲಿ ನೀಡಲಾಗಿದೆ.ನಮ್ಮ 10 ವರ್ಷಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ಕರ್ನಾಟಕಕ್ಕೆ *2,26,837* ಕೋಟಿ ರೂ ಅನುದಾನ ಬಿಡುಗಡೆ ಮಾಡಲಾಗಿದೆ.ಕರ್ನಾಟಕವು *6,280 ಕೋಟಿ* ರೂಪಾಯಿಗಳನ್ನು 50 ವರ್ಷಗಳಿಗೆ ಬಡ್ಡಿ ರಹಿತ ಸಾಲವಾಗಿ ಸ್ವೀಕರಿಸಿದೆ.

description: Sam Pitroda, chairman of the Indian Overseas Congress, has responded to the controversy surrounding his remarks on inheritance tax by saying that his comments were misconstrued to divert attention from core issues. The clarification follows criticism from Bharatiya Janata Party leaders over Pitroda's advocacy for an inheritance tax law in India.The Congress party distanced itself from Pitroda's stance, stating that his views do not always represent the party's position. Pitroda, in an interview with news agency ANI, reportedly highlighted the need for a policy on wealth redistribution and discussed the concept of inheritance tax prevalent in the United States. He explained that in the U.S., a portion of an individual's wealth is taxed upon inheritance, with approximately 55% going to the government and 45% to the heirs.Pitroda expressed his support for such a system, believing it to be a fair way to ensure that wealth is not solely retained within families but also benefits the public.

description: Follow  for more parenting strategies and guidance!I am personally concerned about the current state of the education system. With a daughter approaching the age that she will begin school, I have many reservations.I don’t believe schools have the staff, tools, or capabilities to support the diverse children that they desire to teach. I don’t believe that the curriculum and focus on standardized testing result in the best learning possible for the students. And I don’t really believe that schooling currently creates lifelong learners.I hope that I’m wrong. But this message resonated deeply with me today. Maybe it will resonate with you too.Share this post with another parent who needs to hear it!Speaker: Eddy ZhongSource: How School Makes Kids Less Intelligent | TEDxYouth@BeaconStreet

description: “There are only two tragedies in life: one is not getting what one wants, and the other is getting it. Don’t trap yourself with the desire of wanting. The paradox of getting what you want is that if we base our happiness on the expectation of great things, then achieving it removes the happiness. And you’re left empty and full of desire. That’s what desire truly is: it’s a trap. Humans are walking paradoxes; we want what we can’t have, and when we finally get what we’ve been craving, it’s never enough. Desire is suffering; it is a contract that you make with yourself that you will be unhappy until you get what you want. But some people might say that desire keeps you going, but isn’t it quite sad that you only consider yourself alive until you get what you want? You allow your unlimited desire to cloud your peace.”-Unknown-🎶 my head was empty “i was only temporary”🔥Want to learn how to grow your social media presence on platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and more? Check out my guide through the link in bio.📝

description: BIG 🚨 Why is RBI suddenly doing this?🔴 WHAT HAPPENED?➡️ The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has ordered Kotak Mahindra Bank to stop the following:* Taking on new customers through online or mobile banking.* Issuing any new Credit cards.🔴 WHY IT HAPPENED?* RBI found serious problems with the bank's IT systems, especially in the areas of security and disaster recovery.* These problems caused frequent system outages, including a major one on April 15, 2024, which hurt customers.* The bank failed to fix these problems quickly enough, despite warnings and instructions from the RBI.🔴 WHAT IT MEANS FOR YOU* Existing customers of Kotak Mahindra Bank can still use their accounts and credit cards 💳.* The bank cannot grow its digital customer base until it proves to the RBI that it has fixed its IT problems.**Additional Notes*** The RBI will hire an outside company to audit Kotak Mahindra Bank's IT systems.* Only after the bank fixes the issues found in the audit will the RBI lift the restrictions.* The RBI may take further action against the bank.* If you have a co-branded credit card with Kotak, be aware of this news.

description: Feels like a new beginning 😤Here are two tips to help you stay energetic during your workouts:1. **Stay hydrated**: Drink plenty of water before, during, and after your workout to stay hydrated. Dehydration can lead to fatigue and decreased performance, so it’s essential to replenish fluids regularly.2. **Fuel your body**: Eat a balanced meal or snack containing carbohydrates and protein about 1-2 hours before your workout. description: Decoding tax day 14/30When your salary is 20 lakh rupees it is very difficult to make it to 0, but here is how you can reduce it to minimum amount!Make sure you follow me as I will help you save taxes for sure! 💰1. Gross Salary:₹20,00,000- This is the total income before any deductions or exemptions are applied.2. Allowances and Reimbursements:- House Rent Allowance (HRA): ₹2,00,000- HRA is provided by employers to employees to cover rental expenses. A portion of HRA is exempt from tax.- Leave Travel Allowance (LTA): ₹40,000- LTA covers travel expenses during leave periods and can be tax-exempt based on certain conditions.- Other Reimbursements: ₹24,500- Reimbursements for various expenses, such as medical or fuel expenses, provided by the employer.-Children Education and Hostel Allowance: ₹9,600- Partially exempt allowance for the education and hostel expenses of children.- Standard Deduction: ₹50,000- A fixed deduction allowed to salaried individuals to reduce taxable income.- Professional Tax: ₹2,400- Tax levied by state governments on professional income.3. Deductions:- Section 80C: ₹1,50,000- Deduction for investments in instruments like EPF, PPF, NSC, ELSS, etc.- Section 80D: ₹50,000- Deduction for health insurance premiums paid for self, family, and parents.- Section 80E: ₹22,000- Deduction for interest paid on education loans for higher studies.. Additional Deductions:- Interest on Home Loan EMIs (Section 24b): -₹2,00,000- Deduction allowed for interest paid on home loan EMIs for a self-occupied property.- National Pension Scheme (NPS) Investments (Section 80CCD(1B)): -₹50,000- Deduction allowed for contributions made to NPS over and above the limit specified under Section 80C.Make sure you follow me and stay updated with tax saving options you don’t know about! 💯

description: "Catalyst Service Solutions Pvt Ltd is responsible for providing food items to the canteen of the automobile firm. The firm had given a contract to provide samosas to another subcontracting firm named Manohar Enterprises. On Saturday, some of the automobile firm employees found condoms, gutka and stones in the samosas," he said.After Manohar Enterprises' staffers were questioned over the incident, it was found that two workers, identified as Firoz Shaikh and Vicky Shaikh, had allegedly stuffed condoms, gutka and stones in the samosas, the Chikhli police station official said."We registered a case under IPC section 328 (causing hurt by means of poison) and 120B (criminal conspiracy). The two accused told us they are employees of SRA Enterprises and were sent by its partners to Manohar Enterprises to adulterate food supplied by the latter," he said.SRA Enterprises was removed from the contract earlier after a bandage was found in a snack they had supplied and the partners, identified as Rahim Shaikh, Azhar Shaikh, and Majhar Shaikh, wanted to smear the market reputation of Manohar Enterprises."One person has been arrested. Further probe is underway," he added.
